info.title: Entry API
info.description: With UiTdatabank's Entry API you can create new events, places and organizers, and add extra info to them with specific requests to add/update properties. For example there are operations to add a label, remove a label, add an image, and so on.

id.description: UUID of the created event to use in subsequent requests or to store as a reference in your application.

url.description: URL of the created event to use in subsequent requests or to store as a reference in your application.

commandId.description: ID of the last internal command that was dispatched for this operation. Will be removed in the future.

Bad Request. Possible error types:

post.description:
Creates a new event with the required properties and any additional optional properties.

By default, the new event will be editable and removable by the user or client that the access token used to perform this request belongs to. If you use a user access token, the user for which the token was obtained will see the new event in their dashboard in UiTdatabank and will be able to edit or remove it. If you use a client access token, only API requests with a token for the same client will be able to edit or remove it.

requestBody.description:
The complete details of the new event to create. The schema of the event is the same as the response of the [`GET /events/{eventId}`](./entry.json/paths/~1events~1{eventId}/get) operation.

This request also supports an older deprecated schema that was used to create an event with just its required fields. If you have an existing integration that still uses this schema, you can view it by switching from the `event` schema to `event.post (deprecated)` below.

get.description: Returns the details of the event for the given `eventId`.

put.description:
Updates the event with the given `eventId` by completely overwriting it with the properties in the given JSON. 

<!-- theme: danger -->

> Any existing optional properties on the event that are not included in the update request will be removed from the event when updating the event via this operation.
>
> As an exception, some existing `labels` or `hiddenLabels` may be kept on the event even if they are not included in the update request. For example if they were added via the UiTdatabank UI, or if the client or user making the request does not have sufficient permission to remove some specific labels.

requestBody.description: The complete details of the event to update. The schema of the event is the same as the response of the [`GET /events/{eventId}`](./entry.json/paths/~1events~1{eventId}/get) operation.

204.description: No Content. The event's `workflowStatus` was successfully updated to `DELETED`.

delete.description: Does a soft-delete of the event. The event will continue to exist but it's `workflowStatus` will be changed to `DELETED`. This will remove it from all publication channels.

post.description:
Creates a new event via the historical `/imports/events` URL.

<!-- theme: danger -->

> This operation is deprecated and should not be used in new integrations. Use the [`POST /events`](./entry.json/paths/~1events/post) operation instead to create new events.
>
> Both operations accept the same JSON bodies nowadays, and support creating events with only the required properties or with additional optional properties.
>
> The only difference is that the default `workflowStatus` for events created via [`POST /events`](./entry.json/paths/~1events/post) is `DRAFT`, while new events created via this `POST /imports/events` operation will have the default workflowStatus `READY_FOR_VALIDATION` for backward compatibility with historical integrations.
>
> If you want your new events to also have the workflowStatus `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`, you can use the [`POST /events`](./entry.json/paths/~1events/post) operation and explicitly set the `workflowStatus` property in your JSON body to `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`.

put.description:
Updates the attendance mode of an event. There are three different attendance modes:
- `offline`: the event takes places on a physical location
- `online`: the events takes places on an online location
- `mixed`: the event takes places both on a real location and a online location

When changing from attendance mode online to either offline or mixed it is required to include the location property with the URI or UUID of the (physical) [location](/models/place.json) that the event is taking place at.

requestBody.description: New attendanceMode to set on the event, and optionally a new location (when moving from attendanceMode `online` to `mixed` or `offline`).

put.description:
Updates the calendar information of the given `eventId`. The calendar information will be completely replaced with the new one.

The required properties depend on the `calendarType` property.

| calendarType  | required  | optional  |
|---|---|---|
| single  | subEvent\[0\].startDate, subEvent\[0\].endDate | subEvent\[0\].status, subEvent\[0\].bookingAvailability  |
| multiple  | subEvent\[\*\].startDate, subEvent[\*\].endDate  | subEvent\[\*\].status, subEvent\[\*\].bookingAvailability  |
| periodic  | startDate, endDate  | openingHours, status, bookingAvailability  |
| permanent  |   | openingHours, status, bookingAvailability  |

<!-- theme: warning -->

> If `status` or `bookingAvailability` is missing on the event or a subEvent, it will default to `Available`.
>
> Although the status and bookingAvailability are optional they have a default value of `Available`. When the status and bookingAvailability is not provided an already set value will be overwritten to `Available`!

<!-- theme: success -->

> You can use `single` and `multiple` interchangeably as long as you also include `subEvent` as well. The API will use the correct type based on the number of subEvents inside `subEvent`.

<!-- theme: info -->

> This endpoint also supports a deprecated schema that uses `timeSpans` instead of `subEvent`. The `timeSpans` also have a slightly different structure than `subEvent`. For new integrations, it is recommended to use the schema with the `subEvent` property. 
> 
> For existing integrations the `timeSpans` property will be supported indefinitely for backward compatibility.

200.description: The calendar summary in either plain text or HTML. For example: ``` Van 6 januari 2021 tot 23 juni 2021 (geannuleerd) ``` Or: ``` Van 6 januari 2021 tot 23 juni 2021 (geannuleerd) ```

get.description:
Returns a human-readable summary of the calendar information of the event. Can be configured to return either plain text or HTML, and to switch between four different formats depending on the amount of space you have to display it.

> For backward compatibility this endpoint is also accessible at the old abbreviated `/events/{eventId}/calsum` path.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Deprecated alternative to the `accept` header. Supported for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Defines the size of the summary. Larger summaries contain more detail for events with multiple dates/hours but will also take up more space when shown in a UI. We recommend to use the format `md` for the search results (and `lg` for events with calendarType single), and to always use `lg` for the detailpage. In some cases (e.g. mobile apps) calendar summary `xs` can be useful.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Defines the language that the summary will be written in. Also influences the date/time format used.

parameters.name: hidePast
parameters.description: Will hide past dates in summaries of events with multiple dates. By default, past dates are not excluded from the calendar summary.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: The timezone to format date/times in.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Indicates the expected content-type. Defaults to `text/plain` but can be set to `text/html` for a HTML response.

parameters.name: langCode
parameters.description: Deprecated alternative to the `language` query parameter. Supported for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Deprecated alternative to the `size` query parameter. Supported for backward compatibility.

put.description:
Replaces the list of departure places on the event.

Departure places are schools or other locations from which transport is arranged to bring children to the event (e.g. a walk, a bus, or a bicycle taxi).

Passing an empty array `[]` removes all departure places from the event.

**Validation rules:**

* The event's `audienceType` must be `childrenOnly`. Returns a `400` error if the audience type does not match.
* Each URI must reference an existing place in UiTdatabank. Returns a `400` error listing the non-existent places.

requestBody.description: New list of departure places to set on the event. Departure places previously set on the event but not included in this list will be removed.

put.description:
Adds the given label to the event with the given `eventId`.

If the specified label does not exist yet in UiTdatabank a new label will be created with default visibility and public permissions (usable by anyone), and linked to the event.

The label must be longer than 1 character and shorter than 255 characters. The label can also not contain the semicolon character. It should match the regex `^(?=.{2,255}$)(?=.*\S.*\S.*)[^;]*$`

204.description: No Content. The label has been added successfully.
No Content. The label was deleted from the event.

put.description:
Updates the event's location to a new place based on the given `eventId` and `placeId` in the URL.

If the `eventId` does not exist a `404 Not Found` response will be returned. If the `placeId` does not exist a `400 Bad Request` response will be returned. Otherwise a `204 No Content` will be returned if successful. (See response examples below.)

<!-- theme: danger -->

> The major-info endpoint is deprecated and should not be used in new integrations!

Updates the "major info" of the event with the given `eventId`.

The major info contains:

* `name`: The name of the event in the event's `mainLanguage`, as a string
* `type`: Id of the event's `eventtype` taxonomy `term`, as a string
* `theme` (optional): Id of the event's `theme` taxonomy `term`, as a string
* `location`: Object with the id of the event's location, as a place's uuid (string)
* `calendar`: Object with the event's calendar information (see schema below)

All properties are required (except for `theme`) and will overwrite existing values of these properties on the event. If the event has a `theme` `term` before this update, but there is no `theme` in this major-info update, the `theme` will be removed.

<!-- theme: success -->
> For backward-compatibility with older integrations, this operation can also be requested via `POST /event/{eventId}/major-info`.

204.description: No Content. The major-info has been updated successfully.

patch.description:
Updates the given subEvents on the event with the given `eventId`.

Allows partial updates, omitted properties will be ignored and remain unchanged. Omitted subEvents will also remain unchanged.

Every subEvent to update requires an `id` property that is an integer that corresponds to their index in the list of subEvents on the parent event. For example `0` for the first subEvent, `1` for the second subEvent, and so on.

<!-- theme: warning -->

> Note! If you change the `startDate` of a subEvent, the subEvents will be re-ordered on the parent event afterwards because subEvents are always sorted chronologically.

Only events with calendar type `single` and `multiple` have subEvents, so only events with those calendar types support this endpoint.

204.description: No Content. The subEvents have been updated successfully.

Bad Request. Possible error types:

* https://api.publiq.be/probs/body/missing

* https://api.publiq.be/probs/uitdatabank/calendar-type-not-supported

value.title: Calendar type not supported
value.detail: Not allowed to update subEvents on calendar type: "permanent". Only subEvents on single and multiple calendar types can be updated.

requestBody.description:
The subEvents to update, with the properties to update. 

Each subEvent must have an `id` property to indicate which subEvent should be updated. This `id` is the position of the subEvent in the list of subEvents on the parent event. For example `0` for the first subEvent, `1` for the second subEvent, and so on.

All other properties are optional, and only properties that are included will be updated. No subEvents or properties will be removed.

204.description: No Content

put.description:
Updates the event's type (examples of types are `Concert`, `Opendeurdag`, `Lessenreeks`, and so on) based on the given `eventId` and `termId`.

Terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md). Only terms from the `eventtype` domain can be used as event types.

put.description:
Updates the workflow status of an event. Possible statuses are:

- `DRAFT`: The default status of new events. Events with this status do not appear in online calendars like <https://www.uitinvlaanderen.be> or the search on <https://www.uitdatabank.be>
- `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`: This status means the event has been published, but not approved yet. Most online calendars will already show it, and it will appear in the search on <https://www.uitdatabank.be>
- `APPROVED`: The event has been approved by a moderator. It will appear on all online calendars. You cannot set this status unless you have moderation permissions.
- `REJECTED`: The event has been rejected by a moderator. It will not appear on any online calendars. You cannot set this status unless you have moderation permissions.
- `DELETED`: The event has been deleted. It will not appear on any online calendars.

If you use a user access token, the user for which the token was obtained will see the new place in their dashboard in UiTdatabank and will be able to edit or remove it. If you use a client access token, only API requests with a token for the same client will be able to edit or remove it.

To ensure data integrity and avoid duplication within the system, each place must have a unique combination of the main language title and address.

> For backward compatibility this endpoint is also accessible at the old abbreviated `/places/{placeId}/calsum` path.

In some cases (e.g. mobile apps) calendar summary `xs` can be useful

put.description:
Updates the workflow status of an place. Possible statuses are:

- `DRAFT`: The default status of new places. Places with this status do not appear in online calendars like <https://www.uitinvlaanderen.be> or the search on <https://www.uitdatabank.be>
- `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`: This status means the place has been published, but not approved yet. Most online calendars will already show it, and it will appear in the search on <https://www.uitdatabank.be>
- `APPROVED`: The place has been approved by a moderator. It will appear on all online calendars. You cannot set this status unless you have moderation permissions.
- `REJECTED`: The place has been rejected by a moderator. It will not appear on any online calendars. You cannot set this status unless you have moderation permissions.
- `DELETED`: The place has been deleted. It will not appear on any online calendars.

Event-calendarType.title: event.calendarType
Event-calendarType.description:
Determines how often the [event](./models/event.json) occurs.

Possible values: `single`, `multiple`, `periodic`, `permanent`.

- **single**: For events that take place at one single day (e.g. a concert of Radiohead) and events with only one start and end date (e.g. camping from monday until sunday). **Must** have 1 [subEvent](./models/event-subEvent.json). Will automatically get a [startDate](./models/event-startDate.json) and [endDate](./models/event-endDate.json) based on the `subEvent`.

- **multiple**: For events that take place on several occasions, whether or not contiguous (e.g. a festival). **Must** have 2+ [subEvents](./models/event-subEvent.json). Will automatically get a [startDate](./models/event-startDate.json) and [endDate](./models/event-endDate.json) based on the `subEvents`.

- **periodic**: For events that take places on recurring moments (e.g. a course watercolor painting, each wednesday evening). **Must** have a [startDate](./models/event-startDate.json) and [endDate](./models/event-endDate.json). Can optionally have [openingHours](./models/event-openingHours.json).

- **permanent**: Not recommended for events, consider creating a [place](./models/place.json) instead. Can optionally have [openingHours](./models/event-openingHours.json). [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/calendar-info#calendartype) with more information.

Event-creator.title: event.creator
Event-creator.description:
The unique identifier of the creator of the [event](./models/event.json). Usually a UUID, but can historally be an e-mail address.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the authentication credentials used to create the event.

Event-departurePlaces-put.title: event.departurePlaces.put
Event-departurePlaces-put.description: A list of URIs representing departure places for events with audienceType childrenOnly. Each URI must reference an existing place in UiTdatabank.

allOf.description: A list of URIs representing departure places for this event. Each URI must reference an existing place in UiTdatabank. Only applicable when the event's audienceType is childrenOnly.

description.description: A human-readable description of the [event](./models/event.json) localized in the language from the URL parameter.

Event-description.title: event.description
Event-description.description:
A human-readable, internationalized description of the [event](./models/event.json). 

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/event-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable description in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable description in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable description in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable description in the `en` (English) language.

examples.nl: Voorbeeld beschrijving. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.fr: Description d'exemple. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.de: Beispiel Beschreibung. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.en: Example description. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

Event-endDate.title: event.endDate
Event-endDate.description:
The date & time that the [event](./models/event.json) or [subEvent](./models/event-subEvent.json) ends, as an ISO-8601 date. For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Required on events when using one of the following calendarTypes:

- `single`
- `multiple`
- `periodic`

Always required on subEvents.

Event-faqs-put.title: event.faqs.put
Event-faqs-put.description: A list of frequently asked questions for an event.

nl.question: Is er eten voorzien?
nl.answer: Ja, we hebben verschillend opties, inclusief vegetarisch.

en.question: Will food be provided?
en.answer: Yes, we have various options, including vegetarian.

Event-faqs.title: event-faqs
Event-faqs.description: A list of several FAQ items in various languages

Event-hiddenLabels.title: event.hiddenLabels
Event-hiddenLabels.description: Same as [labels](./models/event-labels.json), but for labels that should not be visible for end users on publication channels and should instead only be used for filtering or other processing.

Event-image-post.title: event.image.post
Event-image-post.description: Describes the request body to add an image to an [event](./models/event.json).

mediaObjectId.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the image.

Event-image-put.title: event.image.put
Event-image-put.description: Describes the request body to update an image at an [event](./models/event.json).

examples.description: Picture of the last publiq event

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the image.

Event-image.title: event.image
Event-image.description: URL to the main image of the [event](./models/event.json). Will be automatically be set by UiTdatabank to the URL of the first image in `mediaObject`.

Event-labels-put.title: event.labels
Event-labels-put.description: A list of labels that can be used to categorize the [event](./models/event.json).

Event-labels.title: event.labels
Event-labels.description:
One or more labels that categorize the [event](./models/event.json). Compared to [terms](./models/event-terms.json), labels do not have a defined set of possible values or a hierarchy.

Labels are allowed to be visible for end users on publication channels.

See [hiddenLabels](./models/event-hiddenLabels.json) for labels that should not be visible on publication channels.

Event-languages.title: event.languages
Event-languages.description:
List of languages that the [event](./models/event.json) is available in. (Not all localizable properties may contain every language though)

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the languages present in localized properties.

See also [completedLanguages](./models/event-completedLanguages.json).

Event-location.title: event.location
Event-location.description: A reference to a [place](./models/place.json) linked to the event. When writing an event it should only contain an `@id` property. When reading an event it will contain all the properties from the [place](./models/place.json). Always required when creating/updating an event, except when using [attendanceMode](./models/event-attendanceMode.json) `online`.

Event-main-image-put.title: event.main.image.put
Event-main-image-put.description: Describes the request body to set an image as the main image of an [event](./models/event.json).

mediaObjectId.description: The id of the image to set as main image on the event.

Event-mainLanguage.title: event.mainLanguage
Event-mainLanguage.description:
The main language that the [event](./models/event.json) is described in. All localized properties like [name](./models/event-name.json) and [description](./models/event-description.json) will be required to have at least a value for this language.

Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

Event-mediaObject.title: event.mediaObject
Event-mediaObject.description: A list of media objects related to the [event](./models/event.json), in reality always images but called mediaObject for backward compatibility. The main image of the event will always be set to the first image in this list when creating/updating an event with multiple images.

Event-modified.title: event.modified
Event-modified.description:
Date formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime, added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the date & time the [event](./models/event.json) was last modified.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Event-name.title: event.name
Event-name.description:
The human-readable, localized name of the [event](./models/event.json).

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/event-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable name in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable name in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable name in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable name in the `en` (English) language.

Event-onlineUrl.title: event.onlineUrl
Event-onlineUrl.description: The url to follow the [event](./models/event.json) online.

Event-openingHours-childcare.description: Indicates when childcare is available on this day. Each property is independent: omitting a property clears any previously set value for it.

start.description: The time at which childcare becomes available on this day, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation). Must be earlier than `opens`.

end.description: The time at which childcare ends on this day, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation). Must be later than `closes`.

Event-openingHours.title: event.openingHours
Event-openingHours.description: A list of multiple open/closed times and weekdays indicating when the event is happening when using the `periodic` calendarType. Each item can optionally specify childcare availability times.

Event-organizer.title: event.organizer
Event-organizer.description: A reference to an [organizer](./models/organizer.json) linked to the event. When writing an event it should only contain an `@id` property. When reading an event it will contain all the properties from the [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

type.description: The term (type) used to categorize the event. Terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md).

id.description: Unique id of the term (type).

Event-priceInfo.title: event.priceInfo
Event-priceInfo.description: A list of tariffs available for tickets/reservations to attend the [event](./models/event.json).

category.description:
Indicates whether this is the base price or a custom tariff.
`base` for the base price, and `tariff` for custom/extra tariffs.  Tariffs with category `uitpas` are automatically added by UiTdatabank and read-only. They will be updated whenever other tariffs change.

price.description: The price of a ticket in this category.

priceCurrency.description: The currency of the price. Currently only `EUR` is supported.

name.description: Name of a tariff inside priceInfo. Required if category is set to `tariff`. For the `base` category UiTdatabank will set a default. Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the `mainLanguage` property. Names must be unique (per language) when using multiple tarrifs.

groupPrice.description: Indicates whether the price applies per participant (false) or as a total cost for all participants (true). Relevant only for educational events(see https://docs.publiq.be/docs/uitdatabank/entry-api/events/creating-a-school-event).

Event-production.description:
The production that the [event](./models/event.json) belongs to.

A production is a group of events that share the same subject but are taking place in different locations and/or are organized by different organizers.

For example a theatre show that is scheduled to be performed at different locations, or a movie that will be screened at different cinema's.
Event-production.title: event.production

id.description: The unique id of the production, formatted as UUID. For example `10ce7cb1-7bc9-4ce4-a256-460f56c49965`.

title.description: The human-readable name of the production. Not localized but in the original language of the events. Automatically added by UiTdatabank.

otherEvents.description: A list of globally unique URIs of other events that are part of this production. See also [@id](./event-@id.json). Automatically added by UiTdatabank.

Event-sameAs.title: event.sameAs
Event-sameAs.description:
One or more URIs that represent the same [event](./models/event.json) on another API or website, not necessarily in JSON-LD format.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on info from other systems.

For example `http://www.uitinvlaanderen.be/agenda/e/test-event/85b04295-479c-40f5-b3dd-469dfb4387b3`.

Event-startDate.title: event.startDate
Event-startDate.description:
The date & time that the [event](./models/event.json) or [subEvent](./models/event-subEvent.json) starts, as an ISO-8601 date. For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Required on events when using one of the following calendarTypes:

- `single`
- `multiple`
- `periodic`

Always required on subEvents.

Event-status.title: event.status
Event-status.description: Indicates if the [event](./models/event.json) is still happening as scheduled or not.

type.description:
One of three possible status types.

- `Available`: Happening or open as scheduled
- `TemporarilyUnavailable`: Postponed or temporarily closed
- `Unavailable`: Cancelled or permanently closed

reason.description: The reason of the status, as a localized human-readable text.

Event-subEvent-bookingAvailability.title: event.subEvent.bookingAvailability
Event-subEvent-bookingAvailability.description: Indicates whether the subevent still has tickets or reservations available. Can contain either a `type` or `remainingCapacity` (or both), and can optionally include capacity information.

Event-subEvent-childcare.description: Indicates when childcare is available during this sub event. Each property is independent: omitting a property clears any previously set value for it.

start.description: The time at which childcare becomes available, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation). Must be earlier than the time portion of `startDate`.

end.description: The time at which childcare ends, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation). Must be later than the time portion of `endDate`.

id.description: Index of the subEvent to update on the parent event. `0` for the first subEvent, `1` for the second event, and so on.

startDate.description: The date & time that the subEvent starts.

endDate.description: The date & time that the subEvent ends.

childcare.description: Childcare information for this sub event. If this property is omitted entirely, the existing childcare data will not be altered. If included, each property present is set and each property omitted is cleared. Send an empty object (`{}`) to clear both `start` and `end`.

status.description: Indicates if the subEvent is still happening as scheduled or not.

type.description:
Required if `status` is included.
One of three possible status types.

- `Available`: The subEvent is happening as scheduled
- `TemporarilyUnavailable`: The subEvent will be postponed
- `Unavailable`: The subEvent is permanently cancelled

reason.description: The reason of the status on the event, as a localized human-readable text.

bookingAvailability.description: Indicates if the subEvent still has tickets or places available for booking.

type.description:
One of two possible booking availability types.

- `Available`: The subEvent still has tickets or places available.
- `Unavailable`: The subEvent has no more tickets or places available.

 Required when `remainingCapacity` is not provided.

capacity.description: Total number of seats or tickets for this subEvent. Can be set independently of `remainingCapacity`.

remainingCapacity.description: Number of remaining seats or tickets for this subEvent. Cannot exceed `capacity` when both are provided. When supplied, `type` is derived automatically: `0` → `Unavailable`, `> 0` → `Available`.

overnight.description: Indicates whether this occurrence involves an overnight stay. Only accepted when the event has a term with id `0.57.0.0.0` ("Kamp of vakantie"). Defaults to `false` when omitted. Automatically reset to `false` when term `0.57.0.0.0` is removed from the event.

Event-subEvent-put.title: event.subEvent.put
Event-subEvent-put.description: A list of occurrences of the [event](./models/event.json) when using the `single` or `multiple` [calendarType](./models/event-calendarType.json).

overnight.description: Indicates whether this occurrence involves an overnight stay. Only accepted when the event has a term with id `0.57.0.0.0` ("Kamp of vakantie"). Defaults to `false` when omitted. Automatically reset to `false` when term `0.57.0.0.0` is removed from the event.

Event-subEvent.title: event.subEvent
Event-subEvent.description: A list of occurrences of the [event](./models/event.json) when using the `single` or `multiple` [calendarType](./models/event-calendarType.json).

id.description: Index of the subEvent on the parent event. `0` for the first subEvent, `1` for the second event, and so on.

overnight.description: Indicates whether this occurrence involves an overnight stay. Only present in the response when `true`. Only applicable when the event has a term with id `0.57.0.0.0` ("Kamp of vakantie").

Event-terms.title: event.terms
Event-terms.description:
A list of taxonomy terms used to categorize the [event](./models/event.json).

Terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md).

An event requires at least one term, to define its type. (For example a concert, theater show, ...)

items.description:
A taxonomy term used to categorize events.

All events require exactly one term of the `eventtype` domain, and can optionally contain other terms.

When reading events, all properties will be available. When creating or updating events only the `id` is required to be included.

id.description: Unique id of the term. For example `0.50.4.0.0`.

label.description: Human-readable label of the term. Currently only available in Dutch. For example `Concert`.

domain.description: The domain of the term. Can be one of `eventtype`, `theme`, or `facility`.

Event-typicalAgeRange-put.title: event.typicalAgeRange.put
Event-typicalAgeRange-put.description: Describes the request body to set the typical age range of an [event](./models/event.json).

typicalAgeRange.description: The typical age range to set on the event.

Event-typicalAgeRange.title: event.typicalAgeRange
Event-typicalAgeRange.description: Indicates the minimum and maximum age to attend the [event](./models/event.json). For example `6-12`, `0-12`, `-12`, or `6-`.

Event-videos-patch.title: event.videos.patch
Event-videos-patch.description: Describes the request body to update one or more videos on an event.

id.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the video to update.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Event-videos-post.title: event.videos.post
Event-videos-post.description: Describes the request body to add a video to an event.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Event-videos.title: event.videos
Event-videos.description: Describes an array of video objects that are linked to an event.

items.description: A video object that is linked to an event or a place.

id.description: The unique id of the video, generated by UiTdatabank.

embedUrl.description: The url that can be used to embed the video inside an iFrame.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Event-with-read-example.title: event
Event-with-read-example.description: A cultural, sport, or pastime activity. For more information: What are events?

Event-with-write-example.title: event
Event-with-write-example.description: A cultural, sport, or pastime activity. For more information: What are events?

availableFrom.description: Can be set when changing the workflowStatus to `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`. The first date & time that the [event](./models/event.json) is allowed to be visible on publication channels. If omitted when changing the workflowStatus to `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`, the current datetime will be used. Must be formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime.

reason.description: Required when setting the workflowStatus to `REJECTED` (only possible with permission to moderate events). The reason why the event was rejected.

Event-workflowStatus.title: event.workflowStatus
Event-workflowStatus.description:
The status in the moderation workflow.

Can be one of four types:

- `DRAFT`: Should not be visible on publication channels yet
- `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`: Submitted for validation. Some publication channels may choose to show these.
- `APPROVED`: Approved, will be visible on all publication channels (depending on the `availableFrom` and `availableTo`). Only possible if you have moderation permissions.
- `REJECTED`: Rejected, will be hidden from all publication channels. Only possible if you have moderation permissions.
- `DELETED`: Removed and should not be visible on any publication channels.

Event.title: event
Event.description: A cultural, sport, or pastime activity. For more information: What are events?

Faq.title: faq
Faq.description: A single FAQ item with translations in one or more languages.

nl.description: The Dutch translation of the FAQ item.

fr.description: The French translation of the FAQ item.

de.description: The German translation of the FAQ item.

en.description: The English translation of the FAQ item.

description.description: Description of the image object, in the language used in the `language` property of the image object. If omitted, the description that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

copyrightHolder.description: Name of the copyright holder of the image. If omitted, the copyright holder that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

inLanguage.description: Locale of text used in the image and its description. If omitted, the language that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

@id.description: The unique URI of the image resource, as included in the response of the image upload request. Not required if `id` is given.

id.description: The unique UUID (generated by UiTdatabank) of the image. Can be used instead of `@id`.

description.description: Description of the image object, in the language used in the `language` property of the image object. If omitted, the description that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

copyrightHolder.description: Name of the copyright holder of the image. If omitted, the copyright holder that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

inLanguage.description: Locale of text used in the image and its description. If omitted, the language that was given when the image was uploaded will be used.

text.description: Textual represenation of the date of the event / place

lg.description: Large date, example: Maandag 28 november 2022 van 20:00 tot 21:00 (Volzet of uitverkocht)

html.description: HTML represenation of the date of the event / place

html.lg: Maandag 28 november 2022 van 20:00 tot 21:00 (Volzet of uitverkocht)

Organizer-@id.title: organizer.@id
Organizer-@id.description: A unique URI that acts as globally unique ID for the [organizer](./models/organizer.json). Based on [JSON-LD node identifiers](https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld/#node-identifiers).

Organizer-address-put.title: organizer.address.put
Organizer-address-put.description: New address of the organizer, localized in a single language.

addressCountry.description: Country code in the ISO-3166 format. For example `BE`.

addressLocality.description: Municipality of the address in the relevant locale, for example `Brussel` for `nl` or `Bruxelles` for `fr`.

postalCode.description: Postal code of the municipality, for example `1000`. Formatted as a string because some international postal codes use letters.

streetAddress.description: Street address in the relevant locale, for example `Wetstraat 1` for `nl` or `Rue de la Loi 1` for `fr`.

Organizer-address.title: organizer.address
Organizer-address.description:
The address the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) is located at. Localized because some parts like the municipality or street address can be different in Dutch, French, German and/or English.

Requires at least one locale, specifically the one defined in the [mainLanguage](./models/organizer-mainLanguage.json) of the organizer.

**Only add a localized version if it's an official variant!**

Organizer-completedLanguages.title: organizer.completedLanguages
Organizer-completedLanguages.description:
List of languages that _every_ localizable property on the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) is available in.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the languages present in localized properties.

See also [languages](./models/organizer-languages.json).

Organizer-contact.title: organizer.contact
Organizer-contact.description:
Contact info of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) in a legacy format, containing one or more phone numbers, email addresses and/or website URLs.

A type is used to categories the kind of contact information. The type can be either: url, email or phone.

type.description: The type of the given contact information.

value.description: The real value of the given contact information.

Organizer-contactPoint-put.title: organizer.contactPoint.put
Organizer-contactPoint-put.description: Contact info containing one or more phone numbers, email addresses and/or website URLs.

phone.description: List of phone numbers for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Phone number for contact purposes.

email.description: List of email addresses for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Email address for contact purposes.

url.description: List of URLs for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: URL to a website for contact purposes.

Organizer-contactPoint.title: organizer.contactPoint
Organizer-contactPoint.description: Contact info of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json), containing one or more phone numbers, email addresses, and/or website URLs. [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/booking-and-contact-info#contactpoint) with more information.

phone.description: List of phone numbers for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Phone number for contact purposes.

email.description: List of email addresses for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Email address for contact purposes.

url.description: List of URLs for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: URL to a website for contact purposes.

Organizer-contributors-put.title: organizer.contributors.put
Organizer-contributors-put.description: An array of emails of users who need to have edit rights.

Organizer-contributors.title: organizer.contributors
Organizer-contributors.description: List of contributors on an [organizer](./models/organizer.json). Only included if you request the organizer with an authenticated user who has the "Aanbod bewerken" permission on the organizer.  [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/contributors) with more information.

Organizer-created.title: organizer.created
Organizer-created.description:
Date formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime, added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the date & time the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) was created at.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Organizer-creator.title: organizer.creator
Organizer-creator.description:
The unique identifier of the creator of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json). Usually a UUID, but can historally be an e-mail address.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the authentication credentials used to create the organizer.

description.description: New description of the organizer, localized in a single language.

Organizer-description.title: organizer.description
Organizer-description.description:
A human-readable, internationalized description of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/organizer-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable description in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable description in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable description in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable description in the `en` (English) language.

examples.nl: Voorbeeld beschrijving. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.fr: Description d'exemple. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.de: Beispiel Beschreibung. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.en: Example description. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

educationalDescription.description: New educational description of the organizer, localized in a single language.

Organizer-educationalDescription.title: organizer.educationalDescription
Organizer-educationalDescription.description:
A human-readable, internationalized educational description of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) that can be used as a description targeted to schools and teachers.

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/organizer-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable description in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable description in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable description in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable description in the `en` (English) language.

examples.nl: Voorbeeld beschrijving. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.fr: Description d'exemple. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.de: Beispiel Beschreibung. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.en: Example description. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

Organizer-geo.title: organizer.geo
Organizer-geo.description: Geographic coordinates corresponding to the [address](./models/organizer-address.json) the organizer is located at. Calculated automatically by UiTdatabank.

latitude.description: A geographic coordinate that specifies the north–south position of a point on the Earth's surface from the equator to one of the poles.

longitude.description: A geographic coordinate that specifies the east–west position of a point on the Earth's surface from Greenwich, England.

Organizer-hiddenLabels.title: organizer.hiddenLabels
Organizer-hiddenLabels.description:
Same as [labels](./models/organizer-labels.json), but for labels that should not be visible for end users on publication channels and should instead only be used for filtering or other processing.

Labels must match the `^(?=.{2,255}$)(?=.*\S.*\S.*)[^;]*$` pattern.

Organizer-image-post.title: organizer.image.post
Organizer-image-post.description: Describes the request body to add an image to an [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

id.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the image.

language.description:
The language of the image or description.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the image.

description.example: Picture of the last publiq event
description.description: Description of the image object, in the language used in the `language` property of the image object.

Organizer-images-patch.title: organizer.images.patch
Organizer-images-patch.description: Describes the request body to update one or more images on an [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

id.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the image.

language.description:
The language of the image or description.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the image.

description.example: Picture of the last publiq event
description.description: Description of the image object, in the language used in the `language` property of the image object.

Organizer-images.title: organizer.images
Organizer-images.description: Describes an array of images that are linked to an [organizer](./models/organizer.json). The main image of the organizer will always be set to the first image in this list when creating/updating an organizer with multiple images.

Organizer-labels-put.title: organizer.labels
Organizer-labels-put.description: A list of labels that can be used to categorize the [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

Organizer-labels.title: organizer.labels
Organizer-labels.description:
One or more labels that categorize the [organizer](./models/organizer.json). Labels do not have a defined set of possible values or a hierarchy.

Labels are allowed to be visible for end users on publication channels.

Labels must match the `^(?=.{2,255}$)(?=.*\S.*\S.*)[^;]*$` pattern.

See [hiddenLabels](./models/organizer-hiddenLabels.json) for labels that should not be visible on publication channels.

Organizer-languages.title: organizer.languages
Organizer-languages.description:
List of languages that the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) is available in. (Not all localizable properties may contain every language though)

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the languages present in localized properties.

See also [completedLanguages](./models/organizer-completedLanguages.json).

imageId.description: The id of the image to set as main image on the organizer.

Organizer-mainImage.title: organizer.mainImage
Organizer-mainImage.description: URL to the main image of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json). Will be automatically be set by UiTdatabank to the URL of the first image in `images`.

Organizer-mainLanguage.title: organizer.mainLanguage
Organizer-mainLanguage.description:
The main language that the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) is described in. All localized properties like [name](./models/organizer-name.json) and [description](./models/organizer-description.json) will be required to have at least a value for this language.

Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

Organizer-modified.title: organizer.modified
Organizer-modified.description:
Date formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime, added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the date & time the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) was last modified.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

name.description: The new name of the organizer (requires at least one character)

Organizer-name.title: organizer.name
Organizer-name.description:
The human-readable, localized name of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/organizer-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable name in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable name in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable name in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable name in the `en` (English) language.

name.description: The name of the organizer, localized in the language specified in the mainLanguage property.

address.description: The address of the organizer, localized in the language specified in the mainLanguage property.

Organizer-url.title: organizer.url
Organizer-url.description: The unique website of the organizer.

Organizer-with-read-example.title: organizer
Organizer-with-read-example.description: An organization that is hosting an event or is the owner of a location. For more information: What are organizers?

Organizer-workflowStatus.title: organizer.workflowStatus
Organizer-workflowStatus.description:
The status of the [organizer](./models/organizer.json) in the moderation workflow.

Can be one of two values:

- `ACTIVE`: The organizer is created and can be used.
- `DELETED`: The organizer was deleted and should not be visible on any publication channels.

Organizer.title: organizer
Organizer.description: An organization that is hosting an event or is the owner of a location. For more information: What are organizers?

Ownership-state.description: The state of an ownership request

Ownership.title: ownership
Ownership.description: An ownership request between an owner and an item

Permission.description: A permission that can be granted to a user

Place-@id.title: place.@id
Place-@id.description: A unique URI that acts as globally unique ID for the [place](./models/place.json). Based on (JSON-LD node identifiers)[https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld/#node-identifiers].

Place-address-put.title: place.address.put
Place-address-put.description: New address of the [place](./models/place.json), localized in a single language.

addressCountry.description: Country code in the ISO-3166 format. For example `BE`.

addressLocality.description: Municipality of the address in the relevant locale, for example `Brussel` for `nl` or `Bruxelles` for `fr`.

postalCode.description: Postal code of the municipality, for example `1000`. Formatted as a string because some international postal codes use letters.

streetAddress.description: Street address in the relevant locale, for example `Wetstraat 1` for `nl` or `Rue de la Loi 1` for `fr`.

Place-address.title: place.address
Place-address.description:
The address the [place](./models/place.json) is located at. Localized because some parts like the municipality or street address can be different in Dutch, French, German and/or English.

Requires at least one locale, specifically the one defined in the [mainLanguage](./models/place-mainLanguage.json) of the place.

**Only add a localized version if it's an official variant!**

availableFrom.description:
The first date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) is allowed to be visible on publication channels.

Formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime. For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-availableFrom.title: place.availableFrom
Place-availableFrom.description:
The first date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) is allowed to be visible on publication channels.

Formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime. For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-availableTo.title: place.availableTo
Place-availableTo.description:
The last date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) is allowed to be visible on publication channels.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on either the [startDate](./models/place-startDate) or [endDate](./models/place-endDate.json) depending on the [event type](./models/place-terms.json).

Formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime. For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-bookingAvailability.title: place.bookingAvailability
Place-bookingAvailability.description: Indicates whether the place still has tickets or reservations available. Currently only contains a `type` which can only be `Available` for places (as opposed to events that can also have `Unavailable` when sold out), as a place can never be completely sold out forever. Can later be expanded with more detailed info.

Place-bookingInfo.title: place.bookingInfo
Place-bookingInfo.description: Booking info of the [place](./models/place.json), containing one or more phone numbers, email addresses, and/or website URLs. [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/booking-and-contact-info#contactpoint) with more information.

url.description: URL to a website for booking purposes.

urlLabel.description: Call-to-action text to show for the link to the booking url. See [our suggested values](./entry-api/shared/booking-and-contact-info#urllabel).

availabilityStarts.description: The date & time when the booking period starts

availabilityEnds.description: The date & time when the booking period ends

Place-calendar-put.title: place.calendar.put
Place-calendar-put.description: Request body to update the calendar information of a place.

Place-calendarSummary.title: place.calendarSummary
Place-calendarSummary.description: Only visible when adding the extra `embedCalendarSummaries` property to the search query. This contains one or more formatted human-readable summaries of the date/time info of the result.

Place-calendarType.title: place.calendarType
Place-calendarType.description:
Determines how long the [place](./models/place) exists.

Possible values: `periodic`, `permanent`.

- **periodic**: For places that only exist for a specific period. **Must** have a [startDate](./models/place-startDate.json) and [endDate](./models/place-endDate.json). Can optionally have [openingHours](./models/place-openingHours.json).

- **permanent**: For places that exist permanently. Can optionally have [openingHours](./models/place-openingHours.json). [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/calendar-info#calendartype) with more information.

Place-completedLanguages.title: place.completedLanguages
Place-completedLanguages.description:
List of languages that _every_ localizable property on the [place](./models/place.json) is available in.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the languages present in localized properties.

See also [languages](./models/place-languages.json).

Place-contactPoint.title: place.contactPoint
Place-contactPoint.description: Contact info of the [place](./models/place.json), containing one or more phone numbers, email addresses, and/or website URLs. [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/booking-and-contact-info#contactpoint) with more information.

phone.description: List of phone numbers for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Phone number for contact purposes.

email.description: List of email addresses for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: Email address for contact purposes.

url.description: List of URLs for contact purposes (can be empty)

items.description: URL to a website for contact purposes.

Place-contributors-put.title: place.contributors.put
Place-contributors-put.description: An array of emails of users who need to have edit rights.

Place-contributors.title: place.contributors
Place-contributors.description: List of contributors on a [place](./models/place.json). Only included if you request the place with an authenticated user who has the "Aanbod bewerken" permission on the place. [Here is a detailed guide](./entry-api/shared/contributors) with more information.

Place-created.title: place.created
Place-created.description:
Date formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime, added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the date & time the [place](./models/place.json) was created at.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-creator.title: place.creator
Place-creator.description:
The unique identifier of the creator of the [place](./models/place.json). Usually a UUID, but can historally be an e-mail address.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the authentication credentials used to create the place.

description.description: A human-readable description of the [place](./models/place.json) localized in the language from the URL parameter.

Place-description.title: place.description
Place-description.description:
A human-readable, internationalized description of the [place](./models/place.json).

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/place-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable description in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable description in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable description in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable description in the `en` (English) language.

examples.nl: Voorbeeld beschrijving. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.fr: Description d'exemple. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.de: Beispiel Beschreibung. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.
examples.en: Example description. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

Place-endDate.title: place.endDate
Place-endDate.description:
The date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) ends when using the `periodic` [calendarType](./models/place-calendarType.json), as an ISO-8601 date.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-geo.title: place.geo
Place-geo.description: Geographic coordinates corresponding to the [address](./models/place-address.json) the place is located at. Calculated automatically by UiTdatabank.

latitude.description: A geographic coordinate that specifies the north–south position of a point on the Earth's surface from the equator to one of the poles.

longitude.description: A geographic coordinate that specifies the east–west position of a point on the Earth's surface from Greenwich, England.

Place-hiddenLabels.title: place.hiddenLabels
Place-hiddenLabels.description: Same as [labels](./models/place-labels.json), but for labels that should not be visible for end users on publication channels and should instead only be used for filtering or other processing.

Place-image-post.title: place.image.post
Place-image-post.description: Describes the request body to add an image to an [place](./models/place.json).

mediaObjectId.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the image.

Place-image-put.title: place.image.put
Place-image-put.description: Describes the request body to update an image at a [place](./models/place.json).

examples.description: Picture of the last publiq event

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the image.

Place-image.title: place.image
Place-image.description: URL to the main image of the [place](./models/place.json). Will be automatically be set by UiTdatabank to the URL of the first image in `mediaObject`.

Place-labels-put.title: place.labels
Place-labels-put.description: A list of labels that can be used to categorize the [place](./models/place.json).

Place-labels.title: place.labels
Place-labels.description:
One or more labels that categorize the [place](./models/place.json). Compared to [terms](./models/place-terms.json), labels do not have a defined set of possible values or a hierarchy.

Labels are allowed to be visible for end users on publication channels.

See [hiddenLabels](./models/place-hiddenLabels.json) for labels that should not be visible on publication channels.

Place-languages.title: place.languages
Place-languages.description:
List of languages that the [place](./models/place.json) is available in. (Not all localizable properties may contain every language though)

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the languages present in localized properties.

See also [completedLanguages](./models/place-completedLanguages.json).

Place-main-image-put.title: place.main.image.put
Place-main-image-put.description: Describes the request body to set an image as the main image of a [place](./models/place.json).

mediaObjectId.description: The id of the image to set as main image on the place.

Place-mainLanguage.title: place.mainLanguage
Place-mainLanguage.description:
The main language that the [place](./models/place.json) is described in. All localized properties like [name](./models/place-name.json) and [description](./models/place-description.json) will be required to have at least a value for this language.

Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

Place-mediaObject.title: place.mediaObject
Place-mediaObject.description: A list of media objects related to the [place](./models/place.json), in reality always images but called mediaObject for backward compatibility. The main image of the place will always be set to the first image in this list when creating/updating a place with multiple images.

Place-modified.title: place.modified
Place-modified.description:
Date formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime, added automatically by UiTdatabank based on the date & time the [place](./models/place.json) was last modified.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-name.title: place.name
Place-name.description:
The human-readable, localized name of the [place](./models/place.json).

Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the [mainLanguage](./models/place-mainLanguage.json) property.

nl.description: A human-readable name in the `nl` (Dutch) language.

fr.description: A human-readable name in the `fr` (French) language.

de.description: A human-readable name in the `de` (German) language.

en.description: A human-readable name in the `en` (English) language.

Place-openingHours.title: place.openingHours
Place-openingHours.description: A list of multiple open/closed times and weekdays indicating when the [place](./models/place.json) is open.

opens.description: Opening time on the related weekdays, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation).

closes.description: Closing time on the related weekdays, in H:MM or HH:MM format (24-hour notation).

dayOfWeek.description: One or more weekdays that the open/closing times are applicable for.

items.description: A single weekday.

Place-organizer.title: place.organizer
Place-organizer.description: A reference to an [organizer](./models/organizer.json) linked to the place. When writing a place it should only contain an `@id` property. When reading a place it will contain all the properties from the [organizer](./models/organizer.json).

type.description: The term (type) used to categorize the place. Terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md).

id.description: Unique id of the term (type).

address.description: The address of the place in the main language.

Place-priceInfo.title: place.priceInfo
Place-priceInfo.description: A list of tariffs available for tickets/reservations to visit the [place](./models/place.json).

category.description:
Indicates whether this is the base price or a custom tariff.
`base` for the base price, and `tariff` for custom/extra tariffs.  Tariffs with category `uitpas` are automatically added by UiTdatabank and read-only. They will be updated whenever other tariffs change.

price.description: The price of a ticket in this category.

priceCurrency.description: The currency of the price. Currently only `EUR` is supported.

name.description: Name of a tariff inside priceInfo. Required if category is set to `tariff`. For the `base` category UiTdatabank will set a default. Requires at least one value, for the language specified in the `mainLanguage` property. Names must be unique (per language) when using multiple tarrifs.

groupPrice.description: Indicates whether the price applies per participant (false) or as a total cost for all participants (true). Relevant only for educational events(see https://docs.publiq.be/docs/uitdatabank/entry-api/events/creating-a-school-event).

Place-sameAs.title: place.sameAs
Place-sameAs.description:
One or more URIs that represent the same [place](./models/place.json) on another API or website, not necessarily in JSON-LD format.

Added automatically by UiTdatabank based on info from other systems.

For example `http://www.uitinvlaanderen.be/agenda/p/test-place/85b04295-479c-40f5-b3dd-469dfb4387b3`.

Place-startDate.title: place.startDate
Place-startDate.description:
The date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) starts when using the `periodic` [calendarType](./models/place-calendarType.json), as an ISO-8601 date.

For example `2021-05-17T22:00:00+00:00`.

Place-status.title: place.status
Place-status.description: Indicates if the [place](./models/place.json) is still open for visitations or not.

type.description:
One of three possible status types.

- `Available`: Happening or open as scheduled
- `TemporarilyUnavailable`: Postponed or temporarily closed
- `Unavailable`: Cancelled or permanently closed

reason.description: The reason of the status, as a localized human-readable text.

Place-terms.title: place.terms
Place-terms.description:
A list of taxonomy terms used to categorize the [place](./models/place.json).

Terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md).

A place requires at least one term, to define its type. (For example a museum, monument, ...)

items.description:
A taxonomy term used to categorize places.

All places require exactly one term of the `eventtype` domain, and can optionally contain other terms.

When reading places, all properties will be available. When creating or updating places only the `id` is required to be included.

id.description: Unique id of the term. For example `0.14.0.0.0`.

label.description: Human-readable label of the term. Currently only available in Dutch. For example `Concert`.

domain.description: The domain of the term. Can be one of `eventtype` or `facility`.

Place-typicalAgeRange-put.title: place.typicalAgeRange.put
Place-typicalAgeRange-put.description: Describes the request body to set the typical age range of a [place](./models/place.json).

typicalAgeRange.description: The typical age range to set on the place.

Place-typicalAgeRange.description: Indicates the minimum and maximum age to visit the [place](./models/place.json). For example `6-12`, `0-12`, `-12`, or `6-`.
Place-typicalAgeRange.title: typicalAgeRange

Place-videos-patch.title: place.videos.patch
Place-videos-patch.description: Describes the request body to update one or more videos on a place.

id.description: The unique id (generated by UiTdatabank) of the video to update.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Place-videos-post.title: place.videos.post
Place-videos-post.description: Describes the request body to add a video to a place.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Place-videos.title: place.videos
Place-videos.description: Describes an array of video objects that are linked to a place.

items.description: A video object that is linked to an event or a place.

id.description: The unique id of the video, generated by UiTdatabank.

embedUrl.description: The url that can be used to embed the video inside an iFrame.

language.description:
The language of the video.
Can be one of `nl`, `fr`, `de`, or `en`.

copyrightHolder.description: The copyright holder of the video source.

Place-with-read-example.title: place
Place-with-read-example.description: A cultural, pastime, or sports-related location that can be visited or where an event can be located at. For more information: What are places?

Place-with-write-example.title: place
Place-with-write-example.description: A cultural, pastime, or sports-related location that can be visited or where an event can be located at. For more information: What are places?

availableFrom.description: Can be set when changing the workflowStatus to `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`. The first date & time that the [place](./models/place.json) is allowed to be visible on publication channels. If omitted when changing the workflowStatus to `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`, the current datetime will be used. Must be formatted as an ISO-8601 datetime.

reason.description: Required when setting the workflowStatus to `REJECTED` (only possible with permission to moderate events). The reason why the event was rejected.

Place-workflowStatus.title: place.workflowStatus
Place-workflowStatus.description:
The status in the moderation workflow.

Can be one of four types:

- `DRAFT`: Should not be visible on publication channels yet
- `READY_FOR_VALIDATION`: Submitted for validation. Some publication channels may choose to show these.
- `APPROVED`: Approved, will be visible on all publication channels (depending on the `availableFrom` and `availableTo`). Only possible if you have moderation permissions.
- `REJECTED`: Rejected, will be hidden from all publication channels. Only possible if you have moderation permissions.
- `DELETED`: Removed and should not be visible on any publication channels.

Place.title: place
Place.description: A cultural, pastime, or sports-related location that can be visited or where an event can be located at. For more information: What are places?

Production-eventIds.description: The event id's that are part of the production
Production-eventIds.title: production.eventIds

USER_ACCESS_TOKEN.description: A user access token, obtained by redirecting the end user to publiq's authorization server to login using the **Authorization Code OAuth Flow**. See the [authentication docs about user access tokens](https://docs.publiq.be/docs/authentication/methods/client-access-token) for more info.

CLIENT_ACCESS_TOKEN.description: A client access token, obtained by exchanging your client id and client secret for a token via an HTTP request to publiq's authorization server using the **Client Credentials OAuth Flow**. See the [authentication docs about client access tokens](https://docs.publiq.be/docs/authentication/methods/user-access-token) for more info.

eventId.name: eventId
eventId.description: Unique id of an event, in the format of a UUID

placeId.name: placeId
placeId.description: Unique id of an place, in the format of a UUID

organizerId.name: organizerId
organizerId.description: Unique id of an organizer, in the format of a UUID

articleId.name: articleId
articleId.description: Unique id of a news article, in the format of a UUID

productionId1.name: productionId1
productionId1.description: Unique id of a production into which another production is merged, in the format of a UUID

productionId2.name: productionId2
productionId2.description: Unique id of a production that is merged into another production, in the format of a UUID

language.name: language
language.description: The language of the request body properties

labelName.name: labelName
labelName.description: The label to add to an event, place or organizer. The label name should be longer than 1 character but shorter than 255 characters. The label name should not contain semicolons.

videoId.name: videoId
videoId.description: Unique id of a video embedded in a place or event, in the format of a UUID

termId.name: termId
termId.description: Unique id of a taxonomy term. Taxonomy terms are pre-defined and can be found using our [guide about taxonomy terms](../docs/taxonomy-api/terms.md).

imageId.name: imageId
imageId.description: The unique uuid of an already uploaded image.

ownershipId.name: ownershipId
ownershipId.description: Unique id of an ownership, in the format of a UUID

Unauthorized.description:
Unauthorized. Your request is missing the required credentials to authenticate. See the Authentication documentation for more info.

* type: https://api.publiq.be/probs/auth/unauthorized
* detail: might contain a developer-readable explanation of the reason

Forbidden.description:
Forbidden. Your request was successfully authenticated but you do not have permission to perform this particular request.

* type: https://api.publiq.be/probs/auth/forbidden
* detail: might contain a developer-readable explanation of the reason

value.title: Forbidden
value.detail: user must be admin of organizer abcd1234

NotFound.description:
The requested resource (URL) could not be found.
This can be due to one of multiple reasons:

* The endpoint has a typo and/or does not exist on the API
* One of the path parameters contains a value that is invalid or does not exist
* One of the required query parameters is missing
* One of the query parameters has an invalid value

The `detail` property of the response should contain more specific information.

The `type` will always be `https://api.publiq.be/probs/url/not-found`.

value.title: URL not found
value.detail: The resource with id "76C6AC08-763C-492E-A68C-CBC43A857229" was not found.
